briefdescription.para: This function initializes a XUsbPs instance/driver.

detaileddescription.para:
The initialization entails:
Initialize all members of the XUsbPs structure.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to XUsbPs instance of the controller.

is a pointer to a XUsbPs_Config configuration structure. This structure will contain the requested configuration for the device. Typically, this is a local structure and the content of which will be copied into the configuration structure within XUsbPs.

is the base address of the device. For systems with virtual memory, this address must be the virtual address of the device. For systems that do not support virtual memory this address should be the physical address of the device. For backwards compatibility NULL may be passed in systems that do not support virtual memory (deprecated).

listitem.para: XST_FAILURE an error occurred during initialization.

simplesect.para: After calling XUsbPs_CfgInitialize() the controller IS NOT READY for use. Before the controller can be used its DEVICE parameters must be configured. See xusbps.h for details.

briefdescription.para: This function performs device reset, device is stopped at the end.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to XUsbPs instance of the controller.

briefdescription.para: This function resets the USB device.

detaileddescription.para: Common functions used for DEVICE/HOST mode.

All the configuration registers are reset to their default values. The function waits until the reset operation is complete or for a certain duration within which the reset operation is expected to be completed.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to XUsbPs instance of the controller.

detaileddescription.para: In order to conserve power, USB devices automatically enter the suspended state when the device has observed no bus traffic for a specified period. When suspended, the USB device maintains any internal status, including its address and configuration. Attached devices must be prepared to suspend at any time they are powered, regardless of if they have been assigned a non-default address, are configured, or neither. Bus activity may cease due to the host entering a suspend mode of its own. In addition, a USB device shall also enter the suspended state when the hub port it is attached to is disabled.

A USB device exits suspend mode when there is bus activity. A USB device may also request the host to exit suspend mode or selective suspend by using electrical signaling to indicate remote wakeup. The ability of a device to signal remote wakeup is optional. If the USB device is capable of remote wakeup signaling, the device must support the ability of the host to enable and disable this capability. When the device is reset, remote wakeup signaling must be disabled.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to XUsbPs instance of the controller.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the USB device has entered Suspend mode successfully

detaileddescription.para: If the USB controller is suspended, its operation is resumed when any non-idle signaling is received on its upstream facing port.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to XUsbPs instance of the controller.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the USB device has Resumed successfully

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to XUsbPs instance of the controller.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the USB device has Resumed successfully

briefdescription.para: This functions sets the controller's DEVICE address.

detaileddescription.para: It also sets the advance bit so the controller will wait for the next IN-ACK before the new address takes effect.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to XUsbPs instance of the controller.
is the Address of the device.

listitem.para: XST_INVALID_PARAM: Invalid parameter passed, e.g. address value too big.
